message:
test(cli): add golden layout checksum infrastructure (bd-17e4.2)

Add layout-level golden checksum system that captures node positions
and edge routes independently of rendering, enabling detection of
non-deterministic or unintended layout changes.

- Canonical layout serialization: sorted nodes by ID, coordinates
  rounded to 6 decimal places, edge routes by index
- FNV-1a checksums stored in JSON golden file
- BLESS_LAYOUT=1 environment variable to regenerate checksums
- Evidence logging with node/edge counts and layout dimensions
- 10-run determinism verification for each golden case
- 8 golden cases: flowchart (simple + cycle), sequence, class,
  state, gantt, pie, malformed recovery

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-17e4","title":"EPIC: Methodology & Infrastructure — Alien CS Graveyard Integration","description":"## Methodology & Infrastructure — Alien CS Graveyard Integration

Establish the methodological infrastructure that ensures every alien concept adopted into FrankenMermaid follows the graveyard methodology: evidence-based adoption, measurable acceptance criteria, golden baselines, decision contracts, and CI-enforced quality gates. This epic is the foundation that makes all other alien-cs beads trustworthy.

## Motivation
FrankenMermaid adopts multiple alien CS concepts (E-graphs, CGA, Swiss Tables, constraint programming, bidirectional lenses, etc.). Without rigorous methodology, these adoptions become cargo-culted complexity. The graveyard methodology requires:
1. **Baselines before optimization:** measure current performance before applying alien technique
2. **Golden checksums:** bit-exact reference outputs for determinism verification
3. **Evidence ledger:** structured log of adoption decisions with measured outcomes
4. **Property testing:** mathematical invariants verified continuously
5. **CI gates:** automated enforcement that prevents regressions
6. **Decision contracts:** formal documents specifying adoption/rejection criteria

## Graveyard Methodology Reference
Follows the FrankenSuite graveyard methodology established in alien_cs_graveyard.md:
- Tier A concepts (score >= 5.0): mandatory adoption with full evidence trail
- Tier B concepts (score 3.0-4.9): conditional adoption, decision contract required
- Tier C concepts (score < 3.0): exploratory only, must prove value before integration

## Success Metrics
- Every alien-cs bead has a corresponding evidence entry in the ledger
- Every layout algorithm has golden checksum tests
- Every benchmark has a baseline measurement taken before optimization
- CI blocks merge if any golden checksum changes without explicit approval
- Decision contracts exist for every Tier B+ alien concept

Dependents: (subtasks to be linked)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:41:03.089481872Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:41:03.089481872Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","evidence","infrastructure","methodology"]}
 {"id":"bd-17e4.1","title":"Establish performance baselines for all layout algorithms before optimization","description":"## Performance Baselines Before Optimization

Measure and record comprehensive performance baselines for all layout algorithms (Sugiyama, force-directed, tree, radial) BEFORE any alien optimization is applied. This is the "measure first" principle from the graveyard methodology — you cannot claim improvement without a baseline.

## Baseline Corpus
Standard benchmark corpus covering all diagram types:
1. **Small:** 10-50 nodes, all diagram types (flowchart, class, sequence, state, etc.)
2. **Medium:** 100-500 nodes, flowchart and class diagrams
3. **Large:** 1000-5000 nodes, synthetic DAGs with controlled density
4. **Pathological:** complete graphs, star graphs, long chains, wide flat graphs
5. **Real-world:** 50+ diagrams extracted from popular open-source repos

## Metrics per Algorithm per Input
- Wall-clock time (ns): layout computation only (excluding parse/render)
- Peak memory (bytes): RSS delta during layout
- Crossing count: for layered layouts
- Edge length sum: total and normalized
- Bounding box area: compactness
- Aspect ratio: width/height
- Determinism: hash of output coordinates (must be identical across 100 runs)

## Implementation
1. Create `baselines/` directory with structured JSON output format.
2. Write baseline measurement harness using criterion.rs for timing.
3. Run baselines on x86_64, aarch64, and wasm32 targets.
4. Store results in `baselines/YYYY-MM-DD_baseline.json` with git commit SHA.
5. Provide comparison tool: `cargo run --bin baseline-compare -- before.json after.json`.
6. Integrate with CI: new baselines auto-generated on each release tag.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Baseline measurements for all 4 layout algorithms on all 5 corpus categories
- [ ] All 7 metrics recorded per algorithm per input
- [ ] Determinism verified (identical hash across 100 runs per input)
- [ ] Results stored in version-controlled JSON with commit SHA
- [ ] Comparison tool produces human-readable delta report
- [ ] CI job generates baselines on release tags","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:41:20.701397982Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:41:20.701397982Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline","measurement","methodology","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17e4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:41:20.701397982Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-17e4.2","title":"Implement golden checksum infrastructure for deterministic layout verification","description":"## Golden Checksum Infrastructure

Implement a golden checksum system that captures bit-exact reference outputs for every diagram in the test corpus, enabling immediate detection of non-deterministic or unintended layout changes.

## Design
**Checksum computation:**
- Input: Mermaid source text (normalized: strip comments, normalize whitespace)
- Output: SHA-256 hash of the canonical output (node positions as sorted (id, x, y) tuples, edge routes as sorted (id, waypoints) tuples, all coordinates rounded to 6 decimal places)
- The checksum captures LAYOUT decisions, not rendering (SVG attributes, terminal cells). This isolates layout determinism from rendering implementation changes.

**Golden file format:**
```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "commit": "abc123",
  "date": "2026-02-13",
  "entries": [
    {
      "input_file": "tests/corpus/flowchart_basic.mmd",
      "input_sha256": "...",
      "layout_algorithm": "sugiyama",
      "output_checksum": "...",
      "node_count": 12,
      "edge_count": 15
    }
  ]
}
```

**Workflow:**
1. Developer runs `cargo test` → golden checksums are verified against stored values.
2. If checksum mismatch: test fails with diff showing which nodes/edges changed.
3. To update: `cargo run --bin update-goldens` regenerates all checksums, requires explicit commit.
4. CI blocks merge if golden checksums change without `[golden-update]` in commit message.

## Implementation
1. Create `fm-test-infra` crate with golden checksum utilities.
2. Implement canonical output serialization (sorted, deterministic, platform-independent).
3. Generate initial golden files for entire test corpus.
4. Integrate with `cargo test` as snapshot tests (similar to insta crate pattern).
5. Provide `--update` flag for intentional updates.
6. CI job that detects golden changes and requires explicit approval.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Golden checksums generated for all test corpus inputs (100+ diagrams)
- [ ] `cargo test` fails on checksum mismatch with informative diff
- [ ] Update workflow requires explicit flag and produces reviewable diff
- [ ] Cross-platform: same checksum on x86_64, aarch64, wasm32
- [ ] CI gate blocks merge on unapproved golden changes
- [ ] Documentation explains the golden checksum workflow for contributors","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:41:35.492708409Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:41:35.492708409Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","golden-checksums","methodology","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17e4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:41:35.492708409Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-17e4.3","title":"Build EvidenceLedger for alien concept adoption tracking","description":"## EvidenceLedger for Alien Concept Adoption Tracking

Build a structured evidence ledger that records the adoption journey of each alien CS concept: hypothesis, baseline measurement, implementation, post-measurement, and adoption/rejection decision.

## Ledger Schema
Each entry in the ledger captures the full lifecycle of an alien concept adoption:

```rust
struct EvidenceEntry {
    concept_id: String,           // e.g., "egraph-crossing-min"
    graveyard_section: String,    // e.g., "§6.6"
    graveyard_score: f64,         // e.g., 3.0
    tier: Tier,                   // A, B, or C

    // Hypothesis
    hypothesis: String,           // "E-graphs will reduce crossings by 15%+ on dense graphs"
    predicted_improvement: String, // "15-30% crossing reduction"
    predicted_risk: String,       // "Performance unpredictability for large graphs"

    // Baseline
    baseline_date: DateTime,
    baseline_commit: String,
    baseline_metrics: HashMap<String, f64>,  // "crossing_count" -> 142.0, "layout_ms" -> 23.5

    // Implementation
    implementation_beads: Vec<String>,  // ["bd-1xma.1", "bd-1xma.2", ...]
    implementation_commit: String,

    // Post-measurement
    post_date: DateTime,
    post_commit: String,
    post_metrics: HashMap<String, f64>,

    // Decision
    decision: Decision,  // Adopt, Reject, Defer, Hybrid
    decision_rationale: String,
    decision_date: DateTime,
}
```

## Implementation
1. Create `evidence/` directory in project root for ledger storage.
2. Implement `EvidenceLedger` type with CRUD operations.
3. Ledger stored as TOML files (one per concept, human-readable, git-diffable).
4. CLI commands: `cargo run --bin evidence -- add/update/report <concept_id>`.
5. Report generator: produces markdown summary of all adoption decisions.
6. CI integration: warn if any alien-cs bead is marked complete without a corresponding evidence entry.
7. Link to bead system: each evidence entry references its implementation beads.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Ledger schema covers full adoption lifecycle (hypothesis → decision)
- [ ] TOML storage format is human-readable and git-diffable
- [ ] CLI tool for adding/updating/querying evidence entries
- [ ] Report generator produces markdown summary table
- [ ] CI warns on alien-cs bead completion without evidence entry
- [ ] Initial entries created for: E-graphs (§6.6), Swiss Tables (§7.7), CGA (§12.11), CP (§9.7), Lenses (§6.2), Incremental Re-Layout (§6.1)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:41:50.548649507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:41:50.548649507Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-cs","evidence-ledger","methodology","tracking"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17e4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:41:50.548649507Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-17e4.4","title":"Integrate proptest property testing for mathematical invariants","description":"## Proptest Property Testing for Mathematical Invariants

Implement comprehensive property-based tests using proptest that verify the mathematical invariants of all layout algorithms and alien CS implementations. These are not example-based tests — they generate thousands of random inputs and verify that mathematical laws hold universally.

## Invariants to Verify

**Layout algorithm invariants:**
1. **Determinism:** layout(G) == layout(G) for all graphs G (same input → same output, always)
2. **Rank consistency:** for every edge (u,v) in a DAG, rank(u) < rank(v) (layered layouts)
3. **Non-overlap:** for all node pairs (i,j), bounding_box(i) ∩ bounding_box(j) = ∅
4. **Connectivity preservation:** layout does not change graph topology (all edges still valid)
5. **Boundedness:** all coordinates within [0, MAX_COORD] (no Infinity, no NaN)

**CGA invariants (§12.11):**
6. **Rotor unitarity:** |R|² = R*R̃ = 1 for all rotors R
7. **Sandwich product idempotency:** R*(R*x*R̃)*R̃ = (R*R)*x*(R*R)̃ (composition law)
8. **Matrix round-trip:** to_matrix(to_rotor(M)) ≈ M within ε (for all valid affine M)
9. **Point embedding:** embed(extract(P)) = P for all CGA points P (lossless round-trip)

**E-graph invariants (§6.6):**
10. **Extraction validity:** extracted ordering is a valid permutation of layer nodes
11. **Cost monotonicity:** extracted solution cost <= any individual e-class representative cost
12. **Budget compliance:** e-graph size never exceeds configured max_enodes

**Lens invariants (§6.2):**
13. **GetPut:** put(s, get(s)) = s (modulo whitespace normalization)
14. **PutGet:** get(put(s, v)) = v (view edits round-trip)

## Implementation
1. Add proptest to fm-core, fm-layout, fm-parser dev-dependencies.
2. Implement `Arbitrary` for `Graph`, `NodeId`, `EdgeId`, `Rotor`, `Multivector`.
3. Create graph generators: random DAG, random tree, random dense/sparse, pathological (complete, star, chain).
4. Write property tests for all 14 invariants above.
5. Configure proptest: 10000 cases per property in CI, 1000 in local dev.
6. Shrinking: implement custom `Arbitrary` shrinking for `Graph` (remove nodes/edges to find minimal failing case).
7. Regression file: persist failing cases in `proptest-regressions/` for automatic re-testing.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 14 invariants have corresponding proptest properties
- [ ] Custom Arbitrary implementations for Graph, Rotor, Multivector
- [ ] Graph generators cover: random DAG, tree, dense, sparse, pathological
- [ ] 10000 cases per property in CI (configurable)
- [ ] Custom shrinking produces minimal failing examples
- [ ] Regression files committed and re-tested automatically
- [ ] Zero property violations on current codebase","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:42:14.993885404Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:47:42.022292135Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["invariants","methodology","property-testing","proptest"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17e4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:42:14.993885404Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-17e4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:47:42.022238936Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-17e4.5","title":"Implement CI quality gates for alien concept adoption","description":"## CI Quality Gates for Alien Concept Adoption

Implement CI pipeline gates that enforce the graveyard methodology: no alien concept integration merges without evidence, no performance regression without justification, no golden checksum changes without approval.

## Gate Definitions

**Gate 1: Golden Checksum Guard**
- Trigger: any change to fm-layout or fm-core
- Check: golden checksums unchanged, OR commit message contains [golden-update] with justification
- Fail action: block merge, link to golden checksum update workflow

**Gate 2: Performance Regression Guard**
- Trigger: any change to fm-layout, fm-core, or fm-parser
- Check: benchmark suite runs, all metrics within 10% of baseline (configurable threshold)
- Fail action: block merge if any metric regresses > 10%, warn if > 5%
- Exception: commit message contains [perf-regression-ok: reason]

**Gate 3: Property Test Guard**
- Trigger: any change to fm-layout, fm-core, fm-parser
- Check: all proptest properties pass with 10000 cases
- Fail action: block merge on any property violation

**Gate 4: Evidence Ledger Guard**
- Trigger: any bead with label "alien-cs" moved to complete status
- Check: corresponding evidence ledger entry exists with post-metrics
- Fail action: warn (not block), link to evidence creation workflow

**Gate 5: Determinism Guard**
- Trigger: any change to fm-layout
- Check: run layout 10 times on corpus, all outputs identical
- Fail action: block merge on non-determinism

**Gate 6: Decision Contract Guard**
- Trigger: any alien-cs epic moved to complete
- Check: decision contract document exists in evidence/ directory
- Fail action: warn (not block)

## Implementation
1. Define gates as GitHub Actions workflow steps (or equivalent CI).
2. Each gate is a separate job for parallel execution.
3. Gate results aggregated into a single merge-blocking status check.
4. Override mechanism: repo admin can force-merge with audit trail.
5. Gate configuration stored in `.ci/quality-gates.toml` (thresholds, exceptions).
6. Dashboard: CI generates a quality summary comment on each PR.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 6 gates implemented and running in CI
- [ ] Gates block merge on failure (except Gate 4 and 6 which warn)
- [ ] Override mechanism exists with audit trail
- [ ] Configuration is in-repo and version-controlled
- [ ] Quality summary comment posted on each PR
- [ ] Gate execution time < 10 minutes total (parallel execution)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:42:30.166665414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:47:42.245108816Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["automation","ci","methodology","quality-gates"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-17e4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:42:30.166665414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-17e4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:47:42.133640448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-17e4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:47:42.245035750Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
